title: 2 PM at the Glass house
titleNative: 午後２時、温室にて
synopsis: A story of two boys is told through an dragonfly specimen. Is the story told from the boy's side? Or is this a dragonfly's dream? Who is the dragonfly? Is he alive or dead? One thing is clear - "his" hands are warm. Graduate Work 2013, Tokyo University of the Arts, Graduate School of Film and New Media, Department of Animation. (Source: Geidai Animation)

title: Thunderbolt Fantasy - Bewitching Melody of the West
titleEnglish: Thunderbolt Fantasy - Bewitching Melody of the West
titleNative: Thunderbolt Fantasy 西幽玹歌
synopsis: Lang Wu Yao is a boy born with a supernatural singing voice. Secluded in the snowy mountains, he is put through severe training by his blind mother. Her wish is for her son's voice to be unrivaled, so that it will be heard by the imperial court. But the intense training results in a tragic accident, where she dies. Left an orphan as a result, Lang wanders the land. His voice is used as a tool by the wicked and greedy, with his heart being ground down in the process. Eventually his strange singing catches the ear of the princess of Xi You, and Lang is elevated to the stage his mother had dreamt of. But what awaits him is a life or death battle performance against other musicians for the amusement of the members of the court. Until one day, Lang learns of the rumors of the Sword Plundering Nemesis, an infamous bandit known through all of Xi You for stealing magical weapons. What he doesn't know is that the next weapon in the sights of this thief has been hidden by the emperor in the same

title: HANA-Doll*: Reinterpretation of Flowering
titleNative: 華Doll*-Reinterpretation of Flowering-
synopsis: Anime adaptation of Hana-Doll*, a 2.5D idol franchise. In the story of the idol project, the "Hana Ningyou Project" (Flower Doll Project) exists to artificially create the "perfect idol" by implanting a special flower seed inside a body. The franchise follows the growth of young men who dedicate their lives to the project. The anime will adapt the "first season" of the drama CD series, titled "Flowering". (Source: Anime News Network, edited)

titleNative: 8月のシンフォニー ―渋谷 2002～2003
synopsis: Based on the autobiography of the singer Kawashima Ai. The name of the book is "Saigo no Kotoba (The Last Words)" and it follows Kawashima Ai's life after her mother's death. The story chronicles her journey to make her dream come true to be a singer and, among other things, how she used to perform for free on Tokyo's streets.

title: Sui Kin Chi Ka Moku Dotten Amen SP Dance PV
titleRomaji: Sui Kin Chi Ka Moku Dotten Amen SP Dance PV
titleNative: 水金地火木土天アーメン SPダンスPV
synonyms: Kimi no Iro Special Dance Video
The Colors Within Special Dance Video
きみの色 劇中歌「水金地火木土天アーメン」描き下ろしSPダンスPV
Amen I'm Going Somewhere Special Dance Video
synopsis: Naoko Yamada-directed anime film Kimi no Iro (The Colors Within) released a special "dance" video – though, it’s a little more on-beat for the creator who enjoys showcasing her characters goofing around and showing their personality through movement. The video contains wholly original footage, presented in a TikTok-like format and follows the three main characters, Kimi, Totsuko and Rui, dancing along to the insert song “Sui Kin Chi Ka Moku Dotten Amen” with moves that are reminiscent of those popular with those who upload short-form dance videos. (Source: Crunchyroll News, edited)

title: Ganbare! Kakure Ase Project
titleNative: がんばれ！隠れ汗 プロジェクト
synopsis: An animated commercial directed by Yoshihiro Kanno for Kao Corporation. The theme song track is "I can" by Sayaka Inoue. Promoting Kao Bioré Sarasara Powder Sheets, the ad stars three high school friends, one a flutist named Mina and the other a burgeoning photographer named Akane. Mina finds herself getting wistful as the school year is drawing to a close but through the encouragement of her friends, she's reinvigorated to make the year the best it can be. (Source: Anime News Network, edited)
